title: Metro An open letter from the heart of Iran to America – Don’t give up on us By California Post Staff Published March 7, 2026, 7:11 p.m
raw_response: {"rating": 4.5, "lean": "Right", "explanation": "This is an opinion piece (open letter) published as editorial content. The author's framing and editorial choices consistently align with right-leaning values, particularly in championing Trump's military intervention, criticizing Obama, endorsing monarchist restoration, and framing left-leaning media negatively. While the piece is primarily a first-person testimony, the editorial decision to publish it and the author's own ideological framing warrant a Right classification. Detailed Analysis: (1) The author frames Trump's military strikes on Iran in overwhelmingly positive terms, describing the bombing as hearing 'our cry for freedom' and stating Trump's attack 'changed everything in favor of the people' — this casts unilateral military intervention in a heroic light aligned with right-leaning foreign policy values. (2) The author directly criticizes Obama for abandoning Iranian protesters, writing 'But Obama chose them,' which is a pointed negative characterization of a Democratic president with no nuance or counterpoint. (3) The author explicitly asks readers to 'support President Trump' — a direct political endorsement in the author's own voice. (4) The author frames left-leaning media negatively, claiming the regime wants civilian casualties specifically so 'left-leaning media starts to pressure for the end of the war,' casting liberal media as unwitting tools of the Iranian regime. (5) The author accepts Trump's visa restrictions on Iranians without criticism, writing that he 'accepted that President Trump's visa policies were to weaken the regime, not hurt ordinary people,' aligning with right-leaning immigration restriction rationales. (6) The author compares Khamenei to Hitler and frames the regime as requiring military rather than diplomatic solutions, aligning with hawkish right-leaning foreign policy positions. (7) The author endorses monarchist restoration under Reza Pahlavi as the path forward, which aligns with conservative/traditional governance values rather than progressive democratic movements."}

parsed_output.explanation: This is an opinion piece (open letter) published as editorial content. The author's framing and editorial choices consistently align with right-leaning values, particularly in championing Trump's military intervention, criticizing Obama, endorsing monarchist restoration, and framing left-leaning media negatively. While the piece is primarily a first-person testimony, the editorial decision to publish it and the author's own ideological framing warrant a Right classification. Detailed Analysis: (1) The author frames Trump's military strikes on Iran in overwhelmingly positive terms, describing the bombing as hearing 'our cry for freedom' and stating Trump's attack 'changed everything in favor of the people' — this casts unilateral military intervention in a heroic light aligned with right-leaning foreign policy values. (2) The author directly criticizes Obama for abandoning Iranian protesters, writing 'But Obama chose them,' which is a pointed negative characterization of a Democratic president with no nuance or counterpoint. (3) The author explicitly asks readers to 'support President Trump' — a direct political endorsement in the author's own voice. (4) The author frames left-leaning media negatively, claiming the regime wants civilian casualties specifically so 'left-leaning media starts to pressure for the end of the war,' casting liberal media as unwitting tools of the Iranian regime. (5) The author accepts Trump's visa restrictions on Iranians without criticism, writing that he 'accepted that President Trump's visa policies were to weaken the regime, not hurt ordinary people,' aligning with right-leaning immigration restriction rationales. (6) The author compares Khamenei to Hitler and frames the regime as requiring military rather than diplomatic solutions, aligning with hawkish right-leaning foreign policy positions. (7) The author endorses monarchist restoration under Reza Pahlavi as the path forward, which aligns with conservative/traditional governance values rather than progressive democratic movements.

explanation: This is an opinion piece (open letter) published as editorial content. The author's framing and editorial choices consistently align with right-leaning values, particularly in championing Trump's military intervention, criticizing Obama, endorsing monarchist restoration, and framing left-leaning media negatively. While the piece is primarily a first-person testimony, the editorial decision to publish it and the author's own ideological framing warrant a Right classification. Detailed Analysis: (1) The author frames Trump's military strikes on Iran in overwhelmingly positive terms, describing the bombing as hearing 'our cry for freedom' and stating Trump's attack 'changed everything in favor of the people' — this casts unilateral military intervention in a heroic light aligned with right-leaning foreign policy values. (2) The author directly criticizes Obama for abandoning Iranian protesters, writing 'But Obama chose them,' which is a pointed negative characterization of a Democratic president with no nuance or counterpoint. (3) The author explicitly asks readers to 'support President Trump' — a direct political endorsement in the author's own voice. (4) The author frames left-leaning media negatively, claiming the regime wants civilian casualties specifically so 'left-leaning media starts to pressure for the end of the war,' casting liberal media as unwitting tools of the Iranian regime. (5) The author accepts Trump's visa restrictions on Iranians without criticism, writing that he 'accepted that President Trump's visa policies were to weaken the regime, not hurt ordinary people,' aligning with right-leaning immigration restriction rationales. (6) The author compares Khamenei to Hitler and frames the regime as requiring military rather than diplomatic solutions, aligning with hawkish right-leaning foreign policy positions. (7) The author endorses monarchist restoration under Reza Pahlavi as the path forward, which aligns with conservative/traditional governance values rather than progressive democratic movements.
